title: Real.
content:
I like a look of agony,
    Because I know it's true;
    Men do not sham convulsion,
    Nor simulate a throe.

    The eyes glaze once, and that is death.
    Impossible to feign
    The beads upon the forehead
    By homely anguish strung.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "Real.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
